---
title: "Elevated Rates of High-Risk HPV+ DNA in IBD Patients"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: right
    echo: false
    css: styles/tables.css
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained-math: true
bibliography: references.bib
---


# Elevated Rates of High-Risk HPV+ DNA in IBD Patients

*Introduction:* IBD patients, especially Ulcerative Colitis patients, have significantly increased rates of anal cancer. ( In Crohn's patients with prior perianal disease, the incidence was even higher - 19.6 per 100k person-years, or 10 times the incidence. ) However, no large-scale study has analyzed HPV rates of IBD patients, nor diagnosis rates of High-Risk HPV+ in anal tissue. We did a retrospective analysis of the total IBD patient population in Epic Cosmos, an international dataset with roughly 3 million IBD patients. 

*Methods:*
